name: name
title: Name
description: The coding name of a LinkML schema.
comments:
This is name formatted in the standard **CamelCase** naming convention.  This format enables software tools to work with it without character processing issues.
A LinkML schema contains classes for describing one or more tables (LinkML classes), fields/columns (slots), and picklists (enumerations). DataHarmonizer can be set up to display each table on a separate tab. A schema can also specify other schemas to import, making their slots, classes, etc. available for reuse.

name: id
title: ID
description: The unique URI for identifying this LinkML schema.
comments: Typically the schema can be downloaded from this URI, but currently it is often left as an example URI during schema development. This semantic metadata helps in the comparison of datasets.

name: description
title: Description
description: The plain language description of this LinkML schema.

name: version
title: Version
description: The semantic version identifier for this LinkML schema.
comments: See https://semver.org/

name: in_language
title: Default language
description: This is the default language (ISO 639-1 Code) that the schema’s table, field and picklist (LinkML class, slot, and enumeration) titles, descriptions and other textual items are in.
comments: This is often “en” for English.

name: locales
title: Translations
description: For multilingual schemas, a list of (ISO 639-1) languages (codes) which translations can be provided for.
comments: Language translations are held in the schema’s extensions.locales data structure.

name: default_prefix
title: Default prefix
description: A prefix to assume all classes and slots can be addressed by.

name: imports
title: Imports
description: A list of linkml:[import name] schemas to import and reuse.
comments: Currently only the default linkml:types is implemented.

name: see_also
title: See Also
description: A delimited list of URLs to supporting documentation; or possibly a local relative file path containing such information.

name: schema_id
title: Schema
description: The coding name of the LinkML schema this prefix is listed in.

name: prefix
title: Prefix
description: The namespace prefix string.

name: reference
title: Reference
description: The URI the prefix expands to.

name: schema_id
title: Schema
description: The coding name of the LinkML schema this table (LinkML class) is contained in.

name: name
title: Coding Name
description: The coding name of this table (LinkML class).
comments:
This is name formatted in the standard **CamelCase** naming convention. This format enables software tools to work with it without character processing issues.
Each table can be displayed in DataHarmonizer in a spreadsheet tab. A table may be visible as a top-level DataHarmonizer template", or it may be a subordinate 1-many table linked to a parent table by a primary key field.

name: title
title: Title
description: The plain language name of this table (LinkML class).

name: description
title: Description
description: The plain language description of this table (LinkML class).

name: version
title: Version
description: A semantic version identifier for this table content.
comments: See https://semver.org/

name: see_also
title: See Also
description: A delimited list of URLs to supporting documentation; or possibly a local file path containing such information.

name: class_uri
title: Table URI
description: A URI for identifying this table's semantic type.
comments: This semantic metadata helps in the comparison of datasets.

name: is_a
title: Is a
description: A parent table (LinkML class) that this table inherits attributes from.

name: tree_root
title: Root Table
description: A boolean indicating whether this is a specification for a top-level data container on which serializations are based.
comments: Only one allowed per Schema. See https://linkml.io/linkml/data/csvs.html

name: schema_id
title: Schema
description: The coding name of the schema that this unique key is in.

name: class_id
title: Class
description: The coding name of the table (LinkML class) that this unique key is in.

name: name
title: Coding Name
description: The coding name of this unique key.

name: unique_key_slots
title: Unique key slots
description: A list of a table’s fields (LinkML class’s slots) that make up this unique key
comments: See https://linkml.io/linkml/schemas/constraints.html

name: description
title: Description
description: The description of this unique key combination.

name: notes
title: Notes
description: Editorial notes about an element intended primarily for internal consumption

name: schema_id
title: Schema
description: The coding name of the schema

name: class_id
title: Table
description: The name of a schema table (LinkML class).
comments: A table field record may detail custom additions to a schema’s field (slot) definition.  Alternately as an “attribute”, a table field record may be entirely custom, and not reference any schema field (slot) at all.

name: name
title: Section name
description: A section name that groups fields as they appear within a table (LinkML Class)

name: rank
title: Ordering
description: An integer which sets the order of this field relative to the others within a given table (LinkML slot's slot_usage rank).  This is a calculated field.  Adjust it by dragging and dropping this row to change order of field with respect to table or table section.

name: schema_id
title: Schema
description: The coding name of the schema that this field (LinkML slot) is contained in.
comments: A schema has a list of fields it defines. A schema can also import other schemas' fields.

name: slot_type
title: Type
description: The type of field (LinkML slot) that this field definition record pertains to: either a schema-level shared field, a schema field reused in a table, or a field only defined in a table.
comments:
In a LinkML schema, a field definition (LinkML slot definition) can exist within one of three contexts:
1) A field which is defined at the schema level, and which can be shared by more than one table (LinkML class).
2) A field which a table reuses from its schema's list of fields. Here a table cannot change any schema-defined attributes of the field; it can only add attribute values for empty attributes.  In LinkML such fields and their customized attributes appear in the table's "slot_usage" list.
3) A field which is named only in a given table, and does not appear or inherit any attributes from the schema. (In LinkML such fields appear only in a table’s “attributes” list.)

name: class_id
title: Table reuse
description: If this field definition row (LinkML slot) details a field’s use in a table (LinkML class), provide the table name.
comments: A table field record may detail custom additions to a schema’s field (slot) definition.  Alternately as an “attribute”, a table field record may be entirely custom, and not reference any schema field (slot) at all.

name: name
title: Coding name
description: The coding name of this field (LinkML slot).
comments:
This name is formatted as a standard lowercase **snake_case** formatted name.
A field can be used in one or more tables. A field can be shown as a visible datatype column in a spreadsheet (DataHarmonizer template) tab, and can define (in its range attribute) a basic number, date, string, picklist (categorical or ordinal), or other custom datatype.  A slot may also have a range pointing to more complex classes.

name: rank
title: Ordering
description: An integer which sets the order of this field (LinkML slot) relative to the others within a given table.  This is the LinkML rank attribute.

name: inlined
title: Inlined
description: Within the context of a JSON table representation, whether a complex (non-literal) field’s (LinkML slot’s) content should be represented directly or just represented by a key to another record.

name: inlined_as_list
title: Inlined as list
description: Within the context of a JSON table representation, whether a complex (non-literal) field’s (LinkML slot’s) content should be represented directly as a JSON list of items.

name: slot_group
title: Field group
description: The name of a grouping to place this field (LinkML slot) within during presentation in a table.

name: slot_uri
title: Slot URI
description: A URI for identifying this field’s (LinkML slot’s) semantic type.

name: title
title: Title
description: The plain language name of this field (LinkML slot).
comments: This can be displayed in applications and documentation.

name: range
title: Range
description: The data type or pick list range or ranges that a field’s (LinkML slot’s) value can be validated by.  If more than one, this appears in the field’s specification as a list of "any of" ranges.

name: unit
title: Unit
description: A unit for a numeric field, expressed in the Unified Code for Units of Measure (UCUM, https://ucum.org/) codes.  A UCUM code can be looked up at https://units-of-measurement.org/

name: required
title: Required
description: A boolean TRUE indicates this field (LinkML slot) is a mandatory data field.
comments: A mandatory data field will fail validation if empty.

name: recommended
title: Recommended
description: A boolean TRUE indicates this field (LinkML slot) is a recommended data field.

name: identifier
title: Identifier
description: A boolean TRUE indicates this field (LinkML slot) is an identifier such that each of its row values must be unique within the table.  In LinkML, “If a slot is declared as an identifier then it serves as a unique key for members of that class. It can also be used for inlining as a dict in JSON serializations.”

name: multivalued
title: Multivalued
description: A boolean TRUE indicates this field (LinkML slot) can hold more than one values taken from its range.

name: minimum_value
title: Minimum value
description: A minimum value which is appropriate for the range data type of the field (LinkML slot).

name: maximum_value
title: Maximum value
description: A maximum value which is appropriate for the range data type of the field (LinkML slot).

name: minimum_cardinality
title: Minimum cardinality
description: For a multivalued field (LinkML slot), a minimum count of values required.

name: maximum_cardinality
title: Maximum cardinality
description: For a multivalued field (LinkML slot), a maximum count of values required.

name: todos
title: Conditional
description: A delimited list of simple conditionals that pertain to this field (e.g. ‘<={today}’ , or ‘<={some_other_field_name}’.  This field may also include “work in progress” notes.

name: pattern
title: Pattern
description: A regular expression pattern used to validate a string field’s (LinkML slot)’s value content.
comments: Regular expressions can begin with ^ to ensure start of string is tested, and $ to ensure up to end is tested.

name: structured_pattern
title: Structured pattern
description: This is a concatenation of regular expression patterns named in the settings table.  Each named expression gets replaced by its regular expression before validating the slot’s string value against it.

name: ifabsent
title: Default value
description: Specify a default value for a field (LinkML slot) using the syntax shown in the examples.
examples: For strings: string(default value);For integer: int(42);For float: float(0.5);For boolean: True;For dates: date("2020-01-31");For datetime: datetime("2020-01-31T12:00:00Z")

name: equals_expression
title: Calculated value
description: Enables inferring (calculating) value based on other field (LinkML slot) values. This is a LinkML-script-processed expression, having the syntax of a python expression.
comments: See https://linkml.io/linkml/schemas/advanced.html#equals-expression

name: aliases
title: Aliases
description: A list of other names that slot can be known by.
comments: See https://linkml.io/linkml/schemas/metadata.html#providing-aliases

name: description
title: Description
description: A plan text description of this field (LinkML slot).

name: comments
title: Comments
description: A free text field for adding other comments to guide usage of this field (LinkML slot).

name: examples
title: Examples
description: A delimited field (LinkML slot) for including examples of string, numeric, date or categorical values.

name: exact_mappings
title: Exact mappings
description: A list of one or more Curies or URIs that point to terms that are semantically identical to this field (LinkML slot).

name: version
title: Version
description: A version number (or date) indicating when this field (LinkML slot) was introduced.

name: notes
title: Notes
description: Editorial notes about this field (LinkML slot) intended primarily for internal consumption

name: schema_id
title: Schema
description: The coding name of the schema this annotation is contained in.

name: annotation_type
title: Annotation on
description: A menu of schema element types this annotation could pertain to (Schema, Class, Slot; in future Enumeration …)

name: class_name
title: On table
description: If this annotation is attached to a table (LinkML class), provide the name of the table.

name: slot_name
title: On field
description: If this annotation is attached to a field (LinkML slot), provide the name of the field.

name: name
title: Key
description: The annotation key (i.e. coding name of the annotation).
comments: This is a lowercase **snake_case** formatted name in the LinkML standard naming convention.

name: value
title: Value
description: The annotation’s value, which can be a string or an object of any kind (in non-serialized data).

name: schema_id
title: Schema
description: The coding name of the schema this pick list (LinkML enum) is contained in.

name: name
title: Name
description: The coding name of this pick list menu (LinkML enum) of terms.
comments: See https://linkml.io/linkml/schemas/enums.html and https://linkml.io/linkml-model/latest/docs/EnumDefinition/

name: title
title: Title
description: The plain language name of this pick list menu (LinkML enum) of terms.
comments:
The plain language "Title" label should mirror the "Name" label, and should use ordinary textual punctuation.

For more information see
https://linkml.io/linkml-model/latest/docs/title/
examples:
- null value menu
- Complications menu
- Host Disease Menu
- host (common name) menu

name: description
title: Description
description: A plain text description of this pick list (LinkML enum) menu.
comments:
Plain language sentence(s) using ordinary textual punctuation.

For more information see
https://linkml.io/linkml-model/latest/docs/description/
examples: NullValueMenu Description: A picklist menu of  International Nucleotide Database Collaboration (INSDC) standardised missing/null value reporting language to be used where a value of an expected format for sample metadata reporting can not be provided. [source: https://ena-docs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/submit/samples/missing-values.html]

name: enum_uri
title: Enum URI
description: A URI for identifying this pick list’s (LinkML enum) semantic type.
comments: This semantic metadata helps in the comparison of datasets.

name: inherits
title: Inherits
description: Indicates that this menu inherits choices from another menu, and includes or excludes other values (for selection and validation).
comments:
Select a menu from the "Inherits" dropdown list or copy and paste the "Picklist Name (CamelCase)" value for the menu you wish to use. Currently you can only inherit from menus listed within the "Picklist" tab of the schema you are editing. If the dropdown list is not appearing try resizing your browser menu.

For more information see
https://linkml.io/linkml-model/latest/docs/inherits/

name: schema_id
title: Schema
description: The coding name of the schema this menu choice's menu (LinkML enum) is contained in.

name: enum_id
title: Enum
description: The coding name of the menu (LinkML enum) that this choice is contained in.
comments:
To edit this value please refer to it within the "Name" column of the "Picklist" tab. Editing it there will change the "Picklist" name without impacting any underlying "Picklist choices". The code name should be formatted in the standard **CamelCase** naming convention. This format enables software tools to work with it without character processing issues.

For more information see
https://linkml.io/linkml/schemas/enums.html
https://linkml.io/linkml-model/latest/docs/EnumDefinition/

name: text
title: Code
description: The code (LinkML permissible_value key) for the menu item choice.
comments: A picklist's codes must be unique. Whether a picklist label or its code is saved in a data file depends on the data file format.  JSON formatted data files hold codes directly, and thus enforce standardization.  CSV, TSV, XLS, and XLSX data formats receive the choice's label.  This enables saving of multilingual data in language of choice.
examples:
- Lower respiratory tract
- Bronchus
- Lung
- Bronchiole

name: is_a
title: Parent
description: The parent term code (in the same enumeration) of this choice, if any.
comments:
Create a picklist hierarchy by declaring code (LinkML permissible_vaue key) that you wish to be the parent of the value. Only refer to a parent class/choice/permissible value that has already been created under the "Class" column within this specific picklist. When no parent class is declared, the associated "class" remains at the top level of the picklist menu.

For more information see
https://linkml.io/linkml-model/latest/docs/is_a/
examples:
Input for "Class: | Parent Class:"
- Class: Lower respiratory tract
- Class: Bronchus | Parent Class: Lower respirtory tract
- Class: Lung | Parent Class: Lower respiratory tract
- Class: Bronchiole | Parent Class: Lung

Resulting Picklist:
- Lower respiratory tract
--- Bronchus
--- Lung
------Bronchiole

name: title
title: title
description: The plain language title of this menu choice (LinkML PermissibleValue) to display. If none, the code will be displayed.
comments: Suggestion: If empty, the "Code" value will be displayed.
examples:
Class: Nasopharynx (NP)
User Interface Label: rhinopharynx

Class: UBERON:0001728
User Interface Label: Nasopharynx (NP)

name: description
title: Description
description: A plain text description of the meaning of this menu choice.
comments:
Plain language sentence(s) using ordinary textual punctuation. If using a URI/CURIE in the "URI" column, it is recommended to use the associated definition. When applicable, indicate the definition source(s) within square brackets "[ ]" after the description.

For more information see
https://linkml.io/linkml-model/latest/docs/description/
examples:
Class: nasopharynx
Description: The section of the pharynx that lies above the soft palate. [UBERON:0001728]

or 

The uppermost of the three regions of the throat (pharynx), situated behind the nasal cavity and extending from the posterior nares to the level above the soft palate. [TFD][VHOG]

name: exact_mappings
title: Code mappings
description: A list of terms from other schemas or terminology systems that mean exactly the same thing as this term.
comments:
This field is used to point to concepts in external standards that are fully equivalent to the current term, helping both people and software systems recognize that they refer to the same idea. It is aligned with skos:exactMatch and inherits its behavior and purpose from mappings.

"A skos:exactMatch link indicates a high degree of confidence that two concepts can be used interchangeably across a wide range of information retrieval applications." - https://www.w3.org/TR/skos-reference/#L4858

For more information see
https://linkml.io/linkml-model/latest/docs/exact_mappings/
examples:
Class: Pacific Biosciences [GENEPIO:0001927]
Mapping: ENA_GSC_MIxS_WW_ERC00023:PACBIO_SMRT

Note: "ENA_GSC_MIxS_WW_ERC00023" is the database and submission type prefix used to identify the target export, while "PACBIO_SMRT" is the export format required for this specific class.

name: meaning
title: Meaning
description: A URI for identifying this choice's semantic type.
comments:
Suggestion: Input a URI or CURIE (Compact uniform resource identifier).

For more information see
https://linkml.io/linkml-model/latest/docs/meaning/
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uniform_Resource_Identifier
https://cthoyt.com/2021/09/14/curies.html

name: notes
title: Notes
description: Editorial notes about an element intended primarily for internal consumption
comments:
Plain language sentence(s), using ordinary textual punctuation, providing organization specific comments (E.g., curation information or schema editor comments).

For more information see
https://linkml.io/linkml-model/latest/docs/notes/
examples: Discuss with schema workgroup whether the we should add the abbreviation "(NP)" to the "User Interface Label" for the "nasopharynx" "Class".

name: schema_id
title: Schema
description: The coding name of the schema this menu inclusion and exclusion criteria pertain to.

name: enum_id
title: Enum
description: The coding name of the menu (enumeration) that this inclusion criteria pertains to.

name: criteria
title: Criteria
description: Whether to include or exclude (minus) the source ontology’s given terms (and their underlying terms, if any).

name: source_ontology
title: Source ontology
description: The URI of the source ontology to trust and fetch terms from.

name: is_direct
title: Directly downloadable
description: Can the vocabulary source be automatically downloaded and processed, or is a manual process involved?

name: source_nodes
title: Top level term ids
description: The selection of term nodes (and their underlying terms) to allow or exclude selections from, and to validate data by.

name: include_self
title: Include top level terms
description: Include the listed selection of items (top of term branch or otherwise) as selectable items themselves.

name: relationship_types
title: Relations
description: The relations (usually owl:SubClassOf) that compose the hierarchy of terms.

name: schema_id
title: Schema
description: The coding name of the schema this setting is contained in.

name: name
title: Name
description: The coding name of this setting, which I can be referenced in a Linkml structured_pattern expression. See https://linkml.io/linkml/schemas/enums.html and https://linkml.io/linkml-model/latest/docs/EnumDefinition/

name: value
title: Value
description: The setting’s value, which is a regular expression that can be used in a LinkML structured_pattern expression field.

name: description
title: Description
description: A plan text description of this setting.

name: description
title: Description
description: A plain text description of this extension.  LinkML extensions allow saving any kind of object as a value of a key.
